name: Moroccan Chickpea Stew with Quinoa
intro: This Moroccan chickpea stew is super flavorful, warm, hearty, easy to make, cheap, and to top it off, really good for you. Give it a try!

ingredients.item: olive oil, or to taste, divided
salt and ground black pepper to taste
garlic, minced, or more to taste
ground black pepper, or to taste
(4.5 ounces) of can chopped green chiles
(15.5 ounces) of can chickpeas, drained and rinsed
chopped fresh cilantro, or to taste

method: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees F (220 degrees C). Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil.
Spread cherry tomatoes on the prepared baking sheet. Drizzle with 1 tablespoon olive oil. Sprinkle salt and pepper over tomatoes and toss to coat evenly.
Roast in the preheated oven until the tomatoes blister and pop and skins start to char, 15 to 20 minutes.

Meanwhile, heat the remaining olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add onion and cook until translucent, 4 to 5 minutes. Add garlic, paprika, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and pepper. Cook until fragrant, 30 to 60 seconds. Add tomatoes and green chiles; bring to a boil. Reduce stew to a simmer and add chickpeas. Add the cherry tomatoes and their juices. Cover and simmer for 30 minutes.
While the stew simmers, bring 3 cups of water and quinoa to a boil in a saucepan. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and simmer until quinoa is tender, 15 to 20 minutes.
Mix Greek yogurt, hummus, 1 tablespoon water, and vinegar together to make the sauce. Season with salt and pepper.
